entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, how do you use "Create vers...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Group Reporting, when you create a new version using the "Create Version with Template" functionality, the system locks the version once it is created. This means:

  1. No Deletion – You cannot delete the version once it has been created.
  2. No Modifications – You cannot change the values entered during the creation process.
  3. Version Management – While you cannot undo a created version, you can still create additional versions if needed.

This does not mean that you can only use one version. You can create multiple versions for different scenarios (e.g., Actuals, Forecasts, Budgets). However, each version is fixed once created, ensuring data consistency for financial consolidation.

For financial consolidation, this means that once a version is generated and used in reports, it remains as a reference point. If adjustments are needed, you would have to create a new version instead of modifying the existing one. This approach ensures auditability and compliance with financial reporting standards.

I hope this will help you answer your questions.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: OPEX Cost centre allocation - Advanced formula clarif...
entry.content:
Hi @Susanne_Helbig ,

Thank you for your prompt response!

I appreciate the information you've shared, but I still have some concerns regarding the handling of dimension members during data processing with the RESULTLOOKUP keyword. Let me consider small set of data as shown below

Using the provided example:

Data( [d/measure] = "Amount" , [d/debitcredit] = "C" ) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/measure] = "Amount" , [d/debitcredit] = "D")

 

The initial dataset is filtered by the RESULTLOOKUP, and while querying the output, I’ve noticed that the records are aggregated at the account level, but not at the Cost Centre level, date etc. Specifically, I see three different accounts getting aggregated, yet we did not specify that account = # in the data keyword. On the other hand, for the cost centre, there are two distinct values (17101750 and 17101753) which are not getting aggregated.

Could you please help me understand how the RESULTLOOKUP and the DATA keywords work together in these contexts? In which scenarios does the aggregation happen across different dimension members, and where does it not? Your clarity on this matter would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks again for your help!

Regards,

Praveen

entry.title: Re: Consumption in bill of material
entry.content: Use an Alternate Unit of Measure for the component: PAA ('Pair') or similar, without decimals so that the system rounds to whole units. Assign it to the component with 1 PAA = 2 PC and set it as Unit of Issue. Then maintain the BOM item with quantity 1 PAA (with regard to the 10 PC Base quantity of the BOM).  Your requirements will be rounded up to Pairs.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to check and display my own cases in SAP for Me -...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

You can use the filter feature in the Case List: select the case status (select all status, or some of the status), select your S-user as the Reporter, or the other filters, e.g. priority, system number, creation date, etc.

Then click Go to display your own cases.

Then go to Personal view and save your settings/filters.

More information about how to use the Case list, kindly refer to KBA 2931236 - How to work with the newly designed Case List - SAP for Me.

Hope the above information helps.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Is it possible to use F4 help event of CL_GUI_ALV_GRID for CL_SALV_TABLE related program
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have created a program where I want to create custom F4 help depending on the line selected. Please find the code below requesting you to please let me know if there is any solution. I have tried to change the field catalog for two fields to make them editable, but that's not working. Please suggest a correction for this, too.

@Sandra_Rossi 

@naimesh_patel 

@former_member182550 

@raymond_giuseppi 

 

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZPPMAT_QRPRINBT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT ZPPMAT_QRPRINBT.
TABLES : MSEG,MKPF,MARA,MAKT.
SELECTION-SCREEN : BEGIN OF BLOCK B1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-H01.
  SELECT-OPTIONS: S_MBLNR FOR MSEG-MBLNR.
SELECTION-SCREEN : END OF BLOCK B1.

CLASS LCL_EVENT_HANDLER DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.


    METHODS:
      ON_LINK_CLICK
        FOR EVENT
        LINK_CLICK OF CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE
        IMPORTING ROW COLUMN.
    METHODS ON_ADDED_FUNCTION
      FOR EVENT IF_SALV_EVENTS_FUNCTIONS~ADDED_FUNCTION OF CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE
      IMPORTING E_SALV_FUNCTION..


ENDCLASS.                    "lcl_event_handler DEFINITION

CLASS LCL_REPORT DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION .
*   ALV reference
    INTERFACES IF_ALV_RM_GRID_FRIEND .
    DATA: LO_FUNCTIONS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_FUNCTIONS_LIST.
    DATA: LO_SELECTIONS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_SELECTIONS.
    DATA: LO_SALV TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_TABLE.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_MKPF,
             MBLNR TYPE MBLNR,
             MJAHR TYPE MJAHR,
             BLDAT TYPE BLDAT,
             BUDAT TYPE BUDAT,
             XBLNR TYPE XBLNR,
           END OF TY_MKPF.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_MSEG,
             MBLNR      TYPE MBLNR,
             MJAHR      TYPE MJAHR,
             WERKS      TYPE MSEG-WERKS,
             MATNR      TYPE MATNR,
             CHARG      TYPE CHARG_D,
             LIFNR      TYPE LIFNR,
             MENGE      TYPE MENGE_D,
             MEINS      TYPE MEINS,
             EBELN      TYPE EBELN,
             ABLAD      TYPE ABLAD,
             VFDAT      TYPE VFDAT,
             HSDAT      TYPE HSDAT,
             BUDAT_MKPF TYPE BUDAT,
           END OF TY_MSEG.

    TYPES:BEGIN OF TY_FINAL,
            CHECK       TYPE YQRSTR-ZCHECK,
            MBLNR       TYPE YQRSTR-MBLNR,
            MJAHR       TYPE MSEG-MJAHR,
            WERKS       TYPE MSEG-WERKS,
            MATNR       TYPE YQRSTR-MATNR,
            MAKTX       TYPE YQRSTR-MAKTX,
            MENGE       TYPE YQRSTR-MENGE,
            MEINS       TYPE YQRSTR-MEINS,
            SKUN        TYPE YQRSTR-SKUN,
            QUAN        TYPE CHAR10,
            CREATED_BY  TYPE NAME1,
            VERIFIED_BY TYPE NAME1,
            BOX_UNIT    TYPE STRING,
          END OF TY_FINAL.

    DATA :IT_FINAL TYPE TABLE OF TY_FINAL,
          WA_FINAL TYPE  TY_FINAL.
    DATA: IT_MSEG    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_MSEG,
          LO_COLUMNS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_COLUMNS,
          LO_COLUMN  TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_COLUMN_TABLE,
          LO_EVENT   TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE.


    TYPES : BEGIN OF TY_MARA,
              MATNR TYPE MARA-MATNR,
              FERTH TYPE MARA-FERTH,
            END OF TY_MARA.
    TYPES : BEGIN OF TY_MAKT,
              MATNR TYPE MAKT-MATNR,
              MAKTX TYPE MAKT-MAKTX,
              SPRAS TYPE MAKT-SPRAS,
            END OF TY_MAKT.

    DATA: IT_MKPF  TYPE TABLE OF  TY_MKPF,
          WA_MKPF  TYPE  TY_MKPF,
          IT_MSEG1 TYPE TABLE OF  TY_MSEG,
          WA_MSEG1 TYPE  TY_MSEG.
    DATA: IT_MARA TYPE TABLE OF TY_MARA,
          IT_MAKT TYPE TABLE OF TY_MAKT.

    DATA:
      LS_KEY    TYPE SALV_S_LAYOUT_KEY,
      LO_LAYOUT TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_LAYOUT.
    METHODS:
      HANDLE_ON_F4
        FOR EVENT ONF4 OF CL_GUI_ALV_GRID
        IMPORTING E_FIELDNAME
                  ES_ROW_NO
                  ER_EVENT_DATA.
    METHODS:
      DOUBLE_CLICK FOR EVENT DOUBLE_CLICK OF CL_GUI_ALV_GRID IMPORTING E_ROW
                                                                       E_COLUMN
                                                                       ES_ROW_NO.
    METHODS: EVH_AFTER_REFRESH FOR EVENT AFTER_REFRESH OF CL_GUI_ALV_GRID IMPORTING SENDER.
    METHODS : GET_DATA_AND_FILL_DATA,
      DISPLAY.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA: HANDLER_ADDED TYPE ABAP_BOOL.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS LCL_REPORT IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD GET_DATA_AND_FILL_DATA .
    SELECT  MBLNR MJAHR BLDAT BUDAT XBLNR FROM MKPF
          INTO TABLE IT_MKPF
          WHERE MBLNR IN S_MBLNR.

    IF IT_MKPF[] IS NOT INITIAL.
      SELECT MBLNR
             MJAHR
             WERKS
             MATNR
             CHARG
             LIFNR
             MENGE
             MEINS
             EBELN
             ABLAD
             VFDAT
             HSDAT
             BUDAT_MKPF
        FROM MSEG
        INTO TABLE IT_MSEG
        FOR ALL ENTRIES IN IT_MKPF
        WHERE MBLNR = IT_MKPF-MBLNR AND
              BWART = '101'.
    ENDIF.
    IF IT_MSEG IS NOT INITIAL.
      SELECT MATNR
             FERTH  FROM MARA INTO TABLE IT_MARA
             FOR ALL ENTRIES IN IT_MSEG WHERE MATNR = IT_MSEG-MATNR ." SKU Value

      SELECT MATNR
             MAKTX
             SPRAS FROM MAKT INTO TABLE IT_MAKT
        FOR ALL ENTRIES IN IT_MSEG WHERE MATNR = IT_MSEG-MATNR
                                     AND SPRAS = SY-LANGU .

    ENDIF.


    LOOP AT IT_MSEG INTO DATA(WA_MSEG).
      WA_FINAL-MBLNR = WA_MSEG-MBLNR.
      WA_FINAL-MJAHR = WA_MSEG-MJAHR.
      WA_FINAL-WERKS = WA_MSEG-WERKS.
      READ TABLE IT_MARA INTO DATA(WA_MARA) WITH KEY MATNR = WA_MSEG-MATNR.
      IF SY-SUBRC = 0 .
        WA_FINAL-MATNR = WA_MSEG-MATNR.
        READ TABLE IT_makt INTO DATA(WA_MAKT) WITH KEY MATNR = WA_MSEG-MATNR .
        IF  SY-SUBRC = 0 .
          WA_FINAL-MAKTX = WA_MAKT-MAKTX.
        ENDIF.
        WA_FINAL-MENGE = WA_MSEG-MENGE.
        WA_FINAL-MEINS = WA_MSEG-MEINS.
        READ TABLE IT_MARA INTO WA_MARA WITH KEY MATNR = WA_FINAL-MATNR .
        IF SY-SUBRC = 0 .
          WA_FINAL-SKUN = WA_MARA-FERTH.
          IF WA_FINAL-SKUN IS NOT INITIAL .
            WA_FINAL-QUAN = WA_FINAL-MENGE / WA_FINAL-SKUN.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
      APPEND WA_FINAL TO IT_FINAL.
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD .
  METHOD DISPLAY.
    TRY.
        CALL METHOD CL_SALV_TABLE=>FACTORY
          IMPORTING
            R_SALV_TABLE = LO_SALV
          CHANGING
            T_TABLE      = IT_FINAL[].

        IF lO_SALV IS BOUND .
          CALL METHOD LO_SALV->SET_SCREEN_STATUS
            EXPORTING
              REPORT        = SY-REPID
              PFSTATUS      = 'ZSTANDARD' "your PF Status name
              SET_FUNCTIONS = LO_SALV->C_FUNCTIONS_ALL.


          LS_KEY-REPORT = SY-REPID.
          LO_LAYOUT = LO_SALV->GET_LAYOUT( ).
          LO_LAYOUT->SET_SAVE_RESTRICTION( IF_SALV_C_LAYOUT=>RESTRICT_NONE ).
          LO_LAYOUT->SET_KEY( LS_KEY ).
          LO_LAYOUT->SET_DEFAULT( ABAP_TRUE ).

          LO_COLUMNS = LO_SALV->GET_COLUMNS( ).
          LO_COLUMNS->SET_OPTIMIZE( 'X' ).
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'CHECK' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Check' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_CELL_TYPE( IF_SALV_C_CELL_TYPE=>CHECKBOX_HOTSPOT ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'QUAN' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Bag Quantity' ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'CREATED_BY' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Inspected By' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_OPTIMIZED(
                VALUE = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE
              ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'VERIFIED_BY' ). " Find the 'MAKTX' column ans change attributes
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Verified By' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_OPTIMIZED(
                VALUE = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE
              ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'BOX_UNIT' ). " Find the 'MAKTX' column ans change attributes
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Box Unit' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_F4(
                VALUE = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE
              ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_OPTIMIZED(
                VALUE = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE
              ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          DATA LO_SETTINGS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_DISPLAY_SETTINGS.
          LO_SETTINGS = LO_SALV->GET_DISPLAY_SETTINGS( ).
          LO_SETTINGS->SET_STRIPED_PATTERN( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).

          DATA: LO_EVENTS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE,
                G_GRID    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_ALV_GRID.
          LO_EVENTS = LO_SALV->GET_EVENT( ).

          DATA: LO_EVENT_HANDLER TYPE REF TO LCL_EVENT_HANDLER.
          CREATE OBJECT LO_EVENT_HANDLER.

          SET HANDLER HANDLE_ON_F4 FOR ALL INSTANCES.
          SET HANDLER DOUBLE_CLICK FOR ALL INSTANCES.
          DATA(SELECTIONS) = LO_SALV->GET_SELECTIONS( ).
          SELECTIONS->SET_SELECTION_MODE(   IF_SALV_C_SELECTION_MODE=>CELL  ). "Single row selection
          SET HANDLER LO_EVENT_HANDLER->ON_LINK_CLICK FOR LO_EVENTS.
          SET HANDLER LO_EVENT_HANDLER->ON_ADDED_FUNCTION FOR LO_EVENTS.

*          DATA :MO_EDIT  TYPE REF TO IF_SALV_GUI_OM_EDIT_RESTRICTED.
*          MO_EDIT = LO_SALV->EXTENDED_GRID_API( )->EDITABLE_RESTRICTED( ).
*
*          TRY.
*              MO_EDIT->SET_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_COLUMNNAME(
*                EXPORTING
*                  COLUMNNAME              = 'VERIFIED_BY'
*                  ALL_CELLS_INPUT_ENABLED = ABAP_TRUE
*              ).
*            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND. " ALV: General Error Class (Checked in Syntax Check)
*          ENDTRY.
*          TRY.
*              MO_EDIT->SET_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_COLUMNNAME(
*                EXPORTING
*                  COLUMNNAME              = 'CREATED_BY'
*                  ALL_CELLS_INPUT_ENABLED = ABAP_TRUE
*              ).
*            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND. " ALV: General Error Class (Checked in Syntax Check)
*          ENDTRY.
          LO_SALV->DISPLAY( ).
        ENDIF .
      CATCH CX_ROOT .

    ENDTRY.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD HANDLE_ON_F4.
    BREAK-POINT.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD DOUBLE_CLICK.
    BREAK-POINT.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD EVH_AFTER_REFRESH.
    CHECK HANDLER_ADDED EQ ABAP_FALSE.
*    SET HANDLER ME->EVH_DEL_CHANGE_SELECTION FOR SENDER.

*    "to use this method you need to inherit from cl_gui_alv_grid or add interface IF_ALV_RM_GRID_FRIEND
*    "to your class
    SENDER->SET_DELAY_CHANGE_SELECTION(
      EXPORTING
        TIME   = 100    " Time in Milliseconds
      EXCEPTIONS
        ERROR  = 1
        OTHERS = 2
    ).
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
*     message id sy-msgid type sy-msgty number sy-msgno
*                with sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    ENDIF.

    SENDER->REGISTER_DELAYED_EVENT(
      EXPORTING
        I_EVENT_ID = SENDER->MC_EVT_DELAYED_CHANGE_SELECT
      EXCEPTIONS
        ERROR      = 1
        OTHERS     = 2
    ).
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
    ENDIF.

    SENDER->GET_FRONTEND_FIELDCATALOG(
      IMPORTING
        ET_FIELDCATALOG = DATA(FCAT)    " Field Catalog
    ).

    "setting editable fields
    ASSIGN FCAT[ FIELDNAME = 'VERIFIED_BY' ] TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<FCAT>).
    IF SY-SUBRC EQ 0.
      <FCAT>-EDIT = ABAP_TRUE.
    ENDIF.

    ASSIGN FCAT[ FIELDNAME = 'CREATED_BY' ] TO <FCAT>.
    IF SY-SUBRC EQ 0.
      <FCAT>-EDIT = ABAP_TRUE.
    ENDIF.

    SENDER->SET_FRONTEND_FIELDCATALOG( IT_FIELDCATALOG = FCAT ).
    SENDER->REGISTER_EDIT_EVENT(
      EXPORTING
        I_EVENT_ID = SENDER->MC_EVT_MODIFIED    " Event ID
*      exceptions
*       error      = 1
*       others     = 2
    ).
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
*     message id sy-msgid type sy-msgty number sy-msgno
*                with sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    ENDIF.

    SENDER->SET_READY_FOR_INPUT(
      I_READY_FOR_INPUT = 1
    ).

    HANDLER_ADDED = ABAP_TRUE.
    SENDER->REFRESH_TABLE_DISPLAY(
*      exporting
*        is_stable      =     " With Stable Rows/Columns
*        i_soft_refresh =     " Without Sort, Filter, etc.
*      exceptions
*        finished       = 1
*        others         = 2
    ).
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
*     message id sy-msgid type sy-msgty number sy-msgno
*                with sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    ENDIF.


  ENDMETHOD.


ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  DATA: LO_REPORT TYPE REF TO LCL_REPORT.
  CREATE OBJECT LO_REPORT.
  LO_REPORT->GET_DATA_AND_FILL_DATA( ).
  LO_REPORT->DISPLAY( ).

CLASS LCL_EVENT_HANDLER IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD ON_LINK_CLICK.
    DATA :MO_EDIT  TYPE REF TO IF_SALV_GUI_OM_EDIT_RESTRICTED.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <LFA_DATA> LIKE LINE OF LO_REPORT->IT_FINAl.
    READ TABLE LO_REPORT->IT_FINAL ASSIGNING <LFA_DATA> INDEX ROW.
    CHECK SY-SUBRC IS INITIAL.
    IF <LFA_DATA>-CHECK IS INITIAL.
      <LFA_DATA>-CHECK = 'X'.
    ELSE.
      CLEAR <LFA_DATA>-CHECK.
    ENDIF.
    LO_REPORT->LO_SALV->REFRESH( ).
  ENDMETHOD.                    "on_link_click
  METHOD ON_ADDED_FUNCTION.
    DATA: IT_BAPIRET2 TYPE TABLE OF BAPIRET2,
          WA_BAPIRET2 TYPE BAPIRET2.

    DATA: LO_GRID TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_ALV_GRID.
    IF LO_GRID IS INITIAL.
      CALL FUNCTION 'GET_GLOBALS_FROM_SLVC_FULLSCR'
        IMPORTING
          E_GRID = LO_GRID.
    ENDIF.

    CALL METHOD LO_GRID->CHECK_CHANGED_DATA.

    LOOP AT LO_REPORT->IT_FINAL  INTO DATA(WA_FINAL) WHERE CHECK = 'X'.
      IF WA_FINAL-CREATED_BY IS  INITIAL .
        WA_BAPIRET2-TYPE ='E'.
        WA_BAPIRET2-MESSAGE = |Please Maintain Created By For Material Doc { WA_FINAL-MBLNR }|.
        APPEND WA_BAPIRET2 TO IT_BAPIRET2 .
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF .
      IF WA_FINAL-VERIFIED_BY IS  INITIAL .
        WA_BAPIRET2-TYPE ='E'.
        WA_BAPIRET2-MESSAGE = |Please Maintain Verified By For Material Doc { WA_FINAL-MBLNR }|.
        APPEND WA_BAPIRET2 TO IT_BAPIRET2 .
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF .
    ENDLOOP.
    "perform something.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: In-House Repair in S/4HANA Service - Start with an Overview
entry.content:
Hi @Archie_Saxena 

deleting or changing an In-House Repair especially regarding sold-to party is currently not possible. However, you can set the wrong in-house repair immediately to completed, and by default completed in-house repairs are not displayed in the result list.

Best Regards,
Udo

entry.title: Re: Handle translations on custom codelist
entry.content:
It looks like your French translations are not being imported correctly in your SAP SDK solution. Since your imports are not being recognized, try verifying that your XLIFF file structure is correct, as any formatting issues can cause SAP to ignore the import. Additionally, clearing the cache and restarting the system might help if cached data is preventing updates from appearing. You can also check whether manually entering the translations via UI works before attempting another import. If the issue persists, consider using an alternative transport method such as SAP Translation Hub instead of the SDK. Checking logs for errors during import can provide insights into the issue. If none of these steps resolve the problem, raising a support ticket with SAP while referencing the note you mentioned might be the best course of action.😊

Regards : multilingua.ae

entry.title: How does a Content Delivery Network (CDN) enhance application performance and user experience?
entry.content:
Hello,
A CDN improves performance by caching and delivering content from edge servers located near users, reducing latency. It decreases server load and accelerates website speed, benefiting applications with global audiences. Employ tools like Cloudflare or Akamai for robust CDN solutions. Adaptive caching strategies further optimize performance, while security features like DDoS protection enhance reliability. By leveraging a CDN, businesses provide faster, more reliable experiences, ensuring greater customer satisfaction and retention. eztag org

Best Regards,
Jacob Small

entry.title: Re: Calculated measure in a hierarchy of measures inside...
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere: Analytical Model with Hierarchical Measures
### Step 1: Create a Graphical View (Data Model)
1. Open SAP Datasphere and navigate to "Data Builder".
2. Click "New Graphical View" and select your source data (e.g., a table containing Gross Sales, Net Sales, and Tax3. Drag the required columns (measures):
- Gross Sales
- Net Sales
- Tax
4. Save the view and expose it for consumption.
### Step 2: Create an Analytical Model
1. Go to "Data Builder" -> "New Analytical Model".
2. Choose the Graphical View you just created as the source.
3. Define measures:
- Gross Sales (Primary Measure)
- Net Sales (Child Measure)
- Tax (Child Measure)
4. Go to "Hierarchy Settings" and create a Level-based Hierarchy.
### Step 3: Configure a Hierarchical Measure
In the Analytical Model settings:
1. Navigate to "Measures" -> "Hierarchy"
2. Create a Hierarchy and define:
- Parent Node: Gross Sales
- Child Nodes: Net Sales, Tax
3. Define aggregation behavior (Summation) to ensure Net Sales + Tax = Gross Sales.
### Step 4: Save and Deploy the Analytical Model
1. Save the analytical model.
2. Deploy the model to make it available for consumption in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) or other reporting tools.
3. Test it in a report to see Gross Sales as an expandable measure, showing Net Sales and Tax underneath.
### Expected Hierarchical Output in Reports
Sales Report View:
Gross Sales: 100,000
- Net Sales: 80,000
- Tax: 20,000
### Next Steps & Enhancements
- Add filters & parameters for different sales regions.
- Connect the model to SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) for visualization.
- Implement currency conversions if required.

i hope this will helps

entry.title: Re: SAC CSS Input Control Properties dont work
entry.content:
Hi Alex,

are you using account input controls or input controls? On account input controls it should work. If you´re using input controls instead, here´s the fitting CSS:

.inputcontrol .sap-custom-input-control-widget {background-color: #ffffff; }
.inputcontrol .sap-custom-input-control-mark-border {border-color: rgb(0,0,0);}

I tested it with both account & standard input controls. For easier recognition, I changed your colors:

/* ACCOUNT Input Control */
.inputcontrol .sap-custom-account-input-control-widget {background-color: #abcdef; }
.inputcontrol .sap-custom-account-input-control-mark-border {border-color: rgb(255,0,0);}

/* Standard Input Control */
.inputcontrol .sap-custom-input-control-widget {background-color: #abcdef; }
.inputcontrol .sap-custom-input-control-mark-border {border-color: rgb(255,0,0);}

 The result is:
Standard & Account Input Controls formatted by CSS

Please note: the ...mark-border CSS does not change the mark border of non-expanded (account) input controls.

Please set answer to accepted, if this answered your question, thanks.
Denis

entry.title: Re: Dump ACCESS_DENIED | cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_do...
entry.content:
Hello @MariaJoãoRocha

Usually when you use the GUI_DOWNLOAD to download a file to local directory, SAP GUI will show an popup to confirm access to parent directory and all the subdirectories. Once the download is initiated and after you select a specific folder in your local machine where you want the file to be saved, this SAP standard popup would appear asking if you allow SAP GUI to download this file to specific directory. If anyone mistakenly clicks "Deny" instead of "Allow", then GUI does not have access to write anything to that folder, hence this ACCESS_DENIED exception will be raised. Just one of many scenarios of failure, only can pinpoint by debugging the standard FM GUI_DOWNLOAD which is called inside this method. 

Be advised, even if user selects "Allow", sometimes due to corporate policy, some critical folders like "C:\" will be protected which could also raise this exception. 

It might be a one off issue where the user selected "Deny" mistakenly and later clicked "Allow" (or) they changed the directory to low critical folders. Also, the popup will have a checkbox "Always Allow" if once selected GUI might not show the popup again so that might also be a reason why you have not been able to reproduce this issue again. 

Either way key thing is to handle the exception correctly. If you have coded the way you showed with all the exceptions corresponding to SUBRC values, then the dump should have never occurred instead it will only set the SY-SUBRC to 15 after the method call. And based on SY-SUBRC you can show the message output. 

Since you have added the exception handling now after the initial dump, you would not be able get the dump anymore, since exceptions are handled now (runtime error occurs only when you do not handle them). But if you add a SY-SUBRC check after the method call, you can see if exceptions are raised in future for a different scenario.

entry.title: Re: Update characteristics configuration using BAPI_CUSTO...
entry.content:
Did you solve this yet?

Few suggestions and possibly one of them may be the answer to your question,

First thing I noticed was the CONFIG_ID field being filled with ITEM_NO which is wrong. Config ID values different and its internally generated with the increment of 1 starting from 000001 for each line item. So, first read the existing configuration using read BAPI and try to find the relevant CONFIG_ID for the given line item using CFGS_REF structure, then use that CONFIG_ID to update specific characteristic.

Next, VC values are always stored in character format with the length of 40 characters. You can cross check this in the BAPI structures BAPICUVAL, where the VALUE field has CHAR 40. So, floating point conversion doesn't seem to be relevant here.

Also, I see you have filled "VKEY" field for "QUOTATION_CFGS_VK" table. What is the reason behind this? Because VKEY is only used create dependencies between characteristic values and the price of the configurable product. If you are only trying to update the VC characteristic "HM_HEIGHT", this VKEY field does not have to be filled. Does this field update also has pricing dependency?

In the BAPI call, you have uncommented many configuration related structures, but are you filling and using all of them? if not I would suggest to comment unnecessary structures and only enable the QUOTATION_CFGS_VALUE since that is the one you need to update the VC value.

After the BAPI call, any messages on the RETURN structure. If so, post that here to see what it returned. Hope you have added BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT after the BAPI call based on LT_RETURN, if not do add it. 

For more in depth analysis, before running the report, turn on the VC configuration trace using T-Code "CUTRC" and once the report is completed, turn the trace off and see the results. It will capture all the update happened to VC during that time. And it might show whether the characteristic value was accepted by VC or if there is any error.

entry.title: Re: find me a bapi/fm to change a order for iw32 t-code
entry.content:
Boa tarde,
Meu nome é Raquel Souza,
sou do Brasil.
Tentarei ajudar você.

Para alterar a ordem do T-code no IW32 (Manutenção de Ordem de Serviço no SAP PM) via BAPI ou Módulo de Função (FM), aqui estão algumas opções que você pode explorar:

🔍 1. Verifique o BAPI Padrão
Infelizmente, a SAP não tem um BAPI oficial para modificar o pedido do T-code diretamente no IW32. No entanto, você pode tentar usar:
👉 BAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAINTAIN

✅ Como usar?
1️⃣ Use esta função para modificar a ordem de manutenção (PM Order).
2️⃣ Preencha os parâmetros corretos com as alterações desejadas.
3️⃣ Chame BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT para confirmar as alterações.

⚙ 2. Módulo de Função Alternativa (FM)
Outra opção é verificar FMs internos como:
👉 IWOL_ORDERS_UPDATE ( AVISO : Use com cautela, pois pode ser uma função interna não documentada).

📌 Passo a passo
🔹 Primeiro, teste no SE37 com uma ordem de teste.
🔹 Sempre valide o comportamento antes de usá-lo em produção.

🛠 3. Melhorias ou saídas de usuário
Se você não encontrar um BAPI/FM para modificar o T-code diretamente, talvez precise de uma melhoria no IW32:

  • User Exit: IWO10009 – Permite validações e modificações no pedido.

  • BADI: BADI_EAM_SINGLELEVEL – Pode ser usado para personalizar processos em ordens de manutenção.

Espero que isso ajude! 🚀

entry.title: SAP Variant Configuration Knowledge Base API with Increased Limits for Variant Table for $skip
entry.content:
Knowledgebases API – Increase Limits of Variant Table API
The $skip parameter of the API endpoint to read variant tables used to have a maximum length of 4 digits, that is, the maximum value used to be 9999. This limit has been increased to 6 digits, which corresponds to a maximum value of 999.999.

This blog explores the significance of the enhancement, background of Variant Table & its practical shown, and the benefits it brings to users.

Where Are Variant Tables Mainly Used?

Variant tables are a crucial component in SAP Variant Configuration, primarily used to store combinations of characteristic values. They play a vital role in various scenarios, including:

  1. Product Customization: Variant tables allow businesses to define and manage complex product configurations. For example, they can specify that a gray car interior is only available if the exterior paint color is blue.
  2. Dependencies Management: These tables simplify the management of dependencies between different product characteristics. By referencing the table in dependencies, the system can check assigned values or infer values based on predefined rules.
  3. Selection Conditions and Constraints: Variant tables are used in selection conditions, preconditions, constraints, and the conditional part of procedures to check and restrict values entered during configuration.
  4. Value Derivation: In procedures and constraints, variant tables can be used to infer values, ensuring that the configuration process adheres to business rules and logic

Why Was This Enhancement Needed?

The $skip parameter in the API endpoint to read variant tables previously had a maximum length of 4 digits, allowing a maximum value of 9999. This limit was sufficient for smaller datasets but posed challenges for businesses dealing with extensive variant configurations. As product complexity and customization options grew, the need to handle larger datasets became imperative. Increasing the limit to 6 digits, corresponding to a maximum value of 999,999, addresses this need by enabling more efficient data handling and retrieval.

$skip more than 6digit with error

How Does the Increased Limit Help?

The increase in the $skip parameter limit brings several benefits to businesses:

  1. Enhanced Data Handling: With the ability to skip up to 999,999 records, businesses can efficiently manage larger datasets with Etag. This is particularly useful for larger customers with extensive product catalogs and complex configurations.
  2. Improved Performance: The increased limit reduces the need for multiple API calls, thereby improving the performance and speed of data retrieval. This enhancement ensures that users can access the required data more quickly and efficiently.
  3. Scalability: As businesses grow and their product offerings expand, the increased limit provides the scalability needed to handle larger volumes of data without compromising on performance.
  4. Flexibility: The ability to handle larger datasets offers greater flexibility in managing variant configurations. Businesses can now accommodate more complex scenarios and configurations, enhancing their ability to meet diverse customer needs


Conclusion

The increase in the $skip parameter limit for the Variant Table API in SAP Variant Configuration is a significant enhancement that addresses the growing needs of businesses dealing with complex product configurations. By enabling more efficient data handling, improving performance, and providing scalability, this enhancement ensures that SAP Variant Configuration remains a robust and flexible solution for managing product variants. As businesses continue to evolve, SAP's commitment to innovation and improvement will undoubtedly support their journey towards greater efficiency and customer satisfaction.

entry.title: Assign users to specific group in SAP IAS via transformation based on SAP SF User attribute
entry.content:
While provisioning users to SAP Identity Authentication (IAS) system, there is a need to automatically assign users to specific group in target system based on user attribute.  

We can read user attribute from source system and can update user to SAP Identity Authentication (IAS) system and assign groups.

This feature is supported for SCIM-based target systems that support PATCH operations, like Identity Authentication version 2. This will help to keep existing group assignments of user in place while assigning new groups.

Prerequisites

If we are using IAS API version 1 then kindly upgrade to IAS API version 2.

Step to update connector version: - Updating a connector version to allow your provisioning system to use a new API.

  1. Change property “ias.api.version ” – “2" - Identity Directory SCIM API 
  2. Reset the system to clear the operational data.
  3. Replace the transformation with the default transformation provided for the respective API version 2.

Context :-

To enable the group assignment, we need to modify the target system transformation by adding a mapping under the user resource containing the following configuration parts:

  • Condition - Defines for which users the condition will apply. For example, all users with emails.
  • Constant - Holds the IDs of the groups in the target system. Currently, we can only identify groups by ID.
  • targetVariable - Specifies whether we want to assign users to groups or unassign users from groups.

Use Case Scenario: -

We are managing users in SAP SuccessFactors (SFSF) and synchronizing them to Identity Authentication service. We’ll show as scenario to assign a specific group to users based on user email domain “abc.com” and “xyz.com”.

  1. Assign user group: - “ABC” to users with email domain as “abc.com”
  2. Assign user group: - “XYZ” to users with email domain other than “abc.com ”

Steps: -

  1. Create Source system as SAP SuccessFactors (SFSF).
  2. Create target system as SAP Identity Authentication (IAS).
  3. In Identity Authentication, create two groups ABC and XYZ once created please note the respective group id. ABC Group ID is bb6cc84e-4587-4031-8045-0ba11f2dff9f and XYZ Group id is 646f00e5-8572-4831-a5c8-a83851b01747.

The group ID that should be specified in the transformation mapping is the SCIM ID of the group resource in IAS SCIM v2 API which is also visible in the SAP Cloud Identity Services administration console under Users & Authorizations -> User Groups -> details of the group

   Group:- ABC 

Group :- XYZ

4. Open the Identity Authentication system, select the Transformations tab and choose Edit and add the following transformations:-

Detail for above transformation

condition 1 mapping:

  • “condition” – Apply this mapping to all users with email domain in SuccessFactors as “ abc.com”
  • “constant” – Assign all the users matching the condition to the given group “ABC” by specifying its group ID.
  • “targetVariable” – Execute assign operation.

condition 2 mapping:

  • “condition” – Apply this mapping to all users with email domain in SuccessFactors as “not equal to abc.com”
  • “constant” – Assign all the users matching the condition to the given group “XYZ” by specifying its group ID.
  • “targetVariable” – Execute assign operation.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           5. Finally, run a provisioning job.

Result :-                    

User with email domain abc.com falling under "condition 1 mapping" gets group “ABC” assigned and user with email domain xyz.com falling under "condition 2 mapping " gets group “XYZ” assigned in SAP IAS.

Group:- ABC

Group:- XYZ

For more information, see

https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/update-connector-version

https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/enabling-group-assignment

entry.title: Best practice when implementing onboarding for multiple countries and employment types
entry.content:
Hi Onboarding Experts,

We’re preparing to implement SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding in our organization, which operates across 7 countries. Within the company, we have three distinctly different employment types:

•White-collar employees
•Blue-collar employees
•A separate group of specialized blue-collar workers

Each of these groups follows a very different onboarding process today. Additionally, there are country-specific requirements and compliance needs to consider.

I’m looking for guidance on best practices:

Is it recommended to configure separate onboarding programs for each country and each employment type? That would technically result in 21 different onboarding programs, which seems quite complex to manage.

How have you handled similar cases? 

I would appreciate any insights, examples from similar implementations.

Thanks!

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Integration with SAP Apps and 3rd Party Apps !
entry.content:
Please refer to Blog 1 (Understanding the Technology Behind SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) - Out of the box 'Insight App") before continuing with this post.

Overview:

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) is a fully managed SaaS solution designed to unify and govern all SAP data, while also enabling seamless integration with third-party data. By combining the capabilities of SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Analytics Cloud, BDC empowers businesses to make real-time, data-driven decisions—supported by secure connectivity and a trusted AI foundation.

I’m excited about BDC and the value it brings. Having experienced firsthand the daily challenges of data transfer from SAP systems to data repositories—such as outdated data, transfer failures, and the lack of end-to-end ownership when issues arise—I understand the critical need for a solution that ensures real-time, up-to-date data availability and especially to leverage AI Insights.

Background:

In Blog 1 (Understanding the Technology Behind SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) - Out of the box 'Insight App"), I provided an overview of BDC and its business benefits. To help you better understand the underlying technologies and concepts, I’ve broken things down into two key scenarios:

  1. Using “Out-of-the-Box” SAP Insights App with SAP S/4HANA and SAP SuccessFactors as source systems—exploring a step-by-step walkthrough of how the process works.
  2. BDC Integration with SAP Applications, Third-Party Applications, and SAP BW/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

This blog dives deeper into Scenario 2.

The architecture below provides a detailed summary of how SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) connects and harmonizes data across SAP and non-SAP applications using a unified data fabric to deliver real-time insights. The key components include data sources, the Business Data Fabric layer, and the consumption layer—enabled through Insight Apps.

Detailed steps:

Step 1: Browse available data products in SAP BDC Cockpit

To extend out-of-the-box Data Products in SAP Business Data Cloud with third-party data, first browse the available Data Products in the SAP Business Data Cloud Cockpit. Select and install the one that best meets your requirements before proceeding with integration.

 

Step 2: Data Product Installation and Transformation Process

When the Data Product installation begins, the bundled business data from your application is accessed, replicated, and processed through various steps within the Foundation Services of SAP Business Data Cloud and transforming it into a ready-to-use Data Product.

Step 3: Data is now available in SAP Datasphere.

Step 4: Integrating Third-Party Data in SAP Datasphere

You can now build the required data model artifacts directly on top of your Data Product in SAP Datasphere. This is where third-party data integration becomes essential. Leveraging SAP Datasphere’s advanced integration and data modeling capabilities, you can seamlessly enrich the out-of-the-box data model with third-party data.

Step 5: Integrating SAP BW/4 HANA Objects into SAP Business Data Cloud

You can also integrate your existing SAP BW/4HANA objects into SAP Business Data Cloud. Using a dedicated data provisioning tool, you can onboard SAP BW objects into SAP Datasphere and seamlessly combine them with the existing data model.

 

Step 6: Reports with AI-Powered Insights

SAP Business Data Cloud offers a variety of consumption options for your data model. One example is the AI-powered Just Ask feature in SAP Analytics Cloud. Leveraging natural language processing, it allows you to retrieve insights simply by typing a question about your data. The system instantly responds, presenting the information in the most suitable format or you can also use Insight App dashboards.

Conclusion:

SAP Business Data Cloud enables businesses to seamlessly integrate, harmonize, and analyze data from multiple sources, transforming raw data into actionable insights. By leveraging AI-driven analytics and automation, organizations can make data-driven decisions with confidence. With its out-of-the-box Insight Apps and seamless integration with SAP solutions, it provides a trusted and unified platform for enterprise data management.

References:

For more details, please see the references below:

SAP Business Data Cloud – Overview

SAP Business Data Cloud – Key Features

SAP & Databricks: A Bold New Era of Data and AI

SAP Business Data Cloud Introduction

SAP Business Data Cloud – Interactive Product Tour

Thank you,
Venu

entry.title: Re: Using class CL_ABAP_PARALLEL for mass parallel dialog work processes
entry.content:
Thanks. In ABAP 7.58, I can see that CL_ABAP_PARALLEL documentation is provided:

  1. via its ABAP Doc comments,
  2. via this short introduction in the SAP Help Portal, Parallel Processing (*), and finally refers to the ABAP Doc comments.

There are also other features in CL_ABAP_PARALLEL:

  • The method RUN_INST can be used instead of RUN. It makes your custom code shorter by avoiding the serialization and deserialization of parameters which are done by CL_ABAP_PARALLEL. The principle of your custom code is to create directly an instance of CL_ABAP_PARALLEL, and each task is to be an instance of a custom class, with attributes for input and result data, implementing the interface IF_ABAP_PARALLEL, which is serializable (IF_SERIALIZABLE_OBJECT) and has the DO method. To start the parallelization, RUN_INST is to be run on the CL_ABAP_PARALLEL instance with the table of your "instance tasks" as input parameter. The result is a copy of your "instance tasks" which contain the results as instance attributes. One example is shown in the ABAP Doc comments of CL_ABAP_PARALLEL.
  • In case your custom application needs to get the results any time before the end of ALL tasks (for instance to achieve high performance in case of chained parallel processes), you  may use the methods FORK, JOIN and IS_COMPLETED. FORK starts one asynchronous task to start all the tasks. JOIN is to get the results of completed tasks, and so JOIN may be called several times till all tasks are completed.
  • There are also the methods FORK_INST and JOIN_INST (same principle as RUN_INST).
  • CONSTRUCTOR has the additional parameter P_ABORT_ON_ERROR to stop starting the remaining tasks if one has heavily failed (RFC communication error, short dump). Technically speaking, the remaining RFC tasks start but end immediately before calling the method DO.
  • CONSTRUCTOR has the additional parameter P_KEEPING_TASKS to not close the RFC connection after each task to manage buffered data. The concept is the same as RECEIVE RESULTS FROM FUNCTION funcname KEEPING TASK. You may close the connection explicitly via the method TERMINATE.
  • There is also the method START_INST_IN_BACKGROUND if you want to use bgRFC.
  • NB: don't use the public methods END_TASK and END_TASK_INT which are reserved to SAP for internal use.

(*) Full path in the SAP Help Portal: Home > SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition > Developer Extensibility > Development in the ABAP Environment > Reuse Services and Libraries > ABAP Language > Parallel Processing)

entry.title: Re: Can we set mandatory for attachments
entry.content:
Yes,

You can do this with an edit with a QBQuery based expression.

NOTE: This Entity Edit may have a significant impact on Posting Performance

1. Create a QBQuery (QBQueryID = 'EE_Test' for this example) with Query Context of Expression with the following SQL (assuming that you want to check for attachments on the Broker record

  select count(e.entityattno)  as num from entityatt e
    inner join broker b on b.brokerno=e.entvalue and e.entname='BROKER'
    and b.brokerno=${brokerno)

2, Create an Entity Edit for the Broker Entity with the following expression  (using the QBQueryId of the Query above)

  att=query('EE_Test',1,BrokerNo)
    if(att > 0 )
    return true 
    else return false

3. This Entity Edit will prevent a Broker record from being saved if there are no attachments for this broker.

Hope this helps!

Phillip Butts

entry.title: Re: Entity Fields - Agent Performance Management
entry.content:
You can use an Entity Edit to prevent changes once the record has been created.

The entity edit expression would look something like:

if(old_BrokerType == BrokerType && //Value that you want base the read only attribute on

old_BrokerRef1 != BrokerRef1). //Value you want to make ready only

return true

else return false

Hope this helps!

Phillip Butts

entry.title: Re: unable to drill down in a Crystal report chart
entry.content:
HI Keith,

I heard back fro Support, try this:

I have heard of one case on Patch 11 where right-click didn’t work in CMC and the plus button also didn’t work.
The cause was found to be a Custom Properties file in C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\tomcat\webapps\BOE\WEB-INF\config\custom and incorrect SAML configuration.

is the failing server an upgraded server?  It seems the one he build for testing works fine?.   Hi can try and see if the right-click context menus don’t work or if folders won’t expand.  If so it may be related to the issue in the viewer.

It’s a long shot but maybe worth checking.

 

https://me.sap.com/notes/3586958

Resolution 1:

  • Review and fix any issues with server.xml (check a working environment). 
  • Test the functionality on HTTP (8080) connector first before testing on SSL (8443) connector. 
  • Before starting the tomcat, Try clearing the tomcat cache by referring: 1879431 - How to clear Tomcat cache? - SAP for Me 
  • Try restarting the tomcat and see that navigation and right click within CMC starts working

Resolution 2:

If Resolution 1 does not work, You can follow the below steps:

  • Stop Tomcat
  • To narrow down the issue, remove any custom properties files.  Navigate to the custom directory EG: C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\tomcat\webapps\BOE\WEB-INF\config\custom and copy the contents inside custom directory to any other location.
  • Remove the existing files in custom directory.
  • Clear the tomcat cache by referring: 1879431 - How to clear Tomcat cache? - SAP for Me 
  • Restart tomcat and see that navigation and right click within CMC starts working.
  • (Optional) After this, if still required, configure SAML by referring to KBA 2791348 - How To: Configure Front-End SAML Authentication on BI 4.3 - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: ABAP OOP Syntax help
entry.content: Class CL_GUI_ALV_GRID_BASE has a method GET_SELECTED_ROWS_ID_BASE which will return the table ROW_TABLE which should contain all the selected rows. ROW_TABLE contains the ROW_ID you are looking for. Use the instance of CL_GUI_ALV_GRID_BASE to call this method to get the ROW_ID. Hope it helps.

entry.title: Re: generating excel with repeated headers from MPP
entry.content:
From limited understanding of your question, here is my suggestion.

When using ABAP2XLSX, anyway you would be looping through item table nested inside the main loop on header table I suppose. For each header record, you can create the heading row in excel followed with header values and one more heading row for items followed with list of item values. Again, in the next main header loop iteration add a new heading row in the excel and so on. 

Maybe if you could provide some sample data of multiple header and line items and also a sample of your planned internal table structure, we can provide better answers.

entry.title: CDS的字段值显示问题
entry.content:
Hi All，

我基于数据源I_SalesDocumentItem 创建了CDS ，场景是外部API。 我选择了元素I_SalesDocumentItem.ShippingPoint，此时，我点击该CDS的按钮数据浏览器，预览数据是正确的。

但是，I_SalesDocumentItem.ShippingPoint只有代码，没有相应的名称，因此，我添加了I_SalesDocumentItem._ShippingPoint._Text.ShippingPointName，这样，我就可以查看名称了，但是，我再次在数据浏览器中查看数据，发现了数据错误，由于ShippingPoint在系统后台中维护了中、英的名称描述，结果，数据重复显示了，一行销售订单项目结果显示了两行。

大家有什么办法吗？要独立再创建一个ShippingPoint的API 的CDS？这样的工作量太大了。还有其他的字段存在同样的情况。

entry.title: Cannot see alternate Unit of Measure quantity in Stock Overview
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

I'd like to see alternate UoM quantity for the product DIGITS in common task Stock Overview.

Please let me know how I can see alternate UoM quantity.

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cannot see alternate Unit of Measure quantity in Stoc...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

In Stock Overview, add field "Alternative Quantity" into the table through Personalization Mode. Then search product DIGITS and select your expected UoM for filter "Alternative Unit of Measure", click Go. In the result, you can find on-hand quantity with that UoM in field "Alternative Quantity".

Henry

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Extractors / CDS-Views for Capex Investment, Cash Flo...
entry.content:
Hi Gilou,

I may not provide the exact CDS view. However I can provide you some general steps to seek for the required CDS view (not only in Cashflow area but also other areas).

  1. Firstly you may go through the blog: Finding the right CDS Extractor in SAP S/4HANA - SAP Community
  2. Secondly, you may search SAP Notes using keywords: CDS extraction Cash Flow. Try to check the notes to see which provides a Cash flow CDS which's extraction-enabled.
  3. Thirdly, you may also check view RSODPABAPCDSEXTR directly in your s/4 system. Display all records and in the result, search using keywords "cash flow". Then check the CDS views one by one to see which one can fulfill your requirement.

Regards,
Maggie

entry.title: SAP TM - Container Unit Creation Question
entry.content:
Hey Community,

I am reaching out to get some guidance on a scenario I am currently working on at work:

We send our PO data to our Freight Forwarders and they send the Container Load Plan back to us, which we then plan in SAP TM (Planning based on actuals). We often have the situation where we have multiple (example three) POs with different destinations. The Freight Forwarder ships those three POs in one container and we organise the transfer of the stock once the container has been unpacked.

In the system we only plan the Freight Unit for the PO that the container gets shipped to and ignore the two Freight Units for the other POs. We now found that the cost distribution is not working as expected, because we do not plan all POs/ Freight Units.

Is there a way to plan the container with all Freight Units without needing to change the POs?

Cheers

Tobias

entry.title: Re: SAP TM - Container Unit Creation Question
entry.content:
Hi @TobiasBauer,

Could you help to answer few questions.

Are you working from Manufacture/OEM/Plant/Shipper or Carrier side? What is PO ? Is this Purchase Order for Fright Forwarder for Transport? Is this Purchase Order for Supplier/Vendor ? How are you planning "plan in SAP TM (Planning based on actuals)" ?

"Is there a way to plan the container with all Freight Units without needing to change the POs?" -> Yes please see the screen shot where you can add multiple FUs. 

Assumption:

Also see the concept of " Consignment Order" ( see click below for details ) where you load cost on different FUs/POs, you do not have to change in PO

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e3dc5400c1cc41d1bc0ae0e7fd9aa5a2/908e8ab6945a47edb1ade56ec6533b2f.html?locale=en-US

#3 FUs=> 1 Container => 1FO => 3 Consignment Orders => Cost distribution on 3 documents...

Kind regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: SAP TM - Container Unit Creation Question
entry.content:
@Ajit_Routray,

Thank you for your response.

I work for the retailer and we use a Freight Forwarder to ship products for us from the supplier to our Warehouse (FOB incoterm).

Yes, PO is the Purchase Order that our Replenishment Team send to the supplier and we also send it to the Freight Forwarder, who utilises a container freight station to optimise the container space. We then get the container load plan back that we plan in SAP TM. The POs get transmitted from S4/HANA to SAP TM and creates the Freight Units (1 FU = 1 PO). We use the Transportation Cockpit to identify the FUs and create the Container Unit, Freight Booking & Freight Order. The Freight Unit get created based on the PO (Order-based Transportation Requirements) hence we have three Freight Units/ POs with different destination locations, which I want to combine in one Container Unit.

I did a test where I selected all three FUs and created the Container Unit, but then the Container Unit has multiple stages. Usually we have two stages Port to Port and Port to Warehouse. 

Can I adjust the FUs or the Container Unit to just one destination? 

Thanks - I will review the consignment order concept. 🙂

Tobias

entry.title: Re: How to Approve/Reject Task via API in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
@padma_kumar_perumal Can you provide more information, for example:

  • Are you using Public or other environment?
  • How are you making the call?
  • Are you using a destination? If so, how is it configured?
  • Are your roles given to your default user or custom user?
  • How did you trigger the process? And whose credentials were used?

On my trial account, I created a new process with API trigger, deployed to a custom environment, then through Postman I was able to retrieve the context and logs using just the API key info for the service.

I am assuming you are doing something different since using just the ID/secret to connect would not involve any "user".
